evidence: Docstring names 'The **Inverse Galois Problem**: every finite group is isomorphic to the Galois group of a Galois extension of the rationals', and the file header references the Wikipedia article Inverse Galois problem.
The statement asks whether every finite group 'is isomorphic to the Galois group of a Galois extension of the rationals', via the GaloisRealization structure's iso to (L ≃ₐ[K] L).
The conjecture quantifies over 'every finite group' ([Fintype G] [Group G]) as the object to be realized as a Galois group.
Docstring asks 'Are e and pi algebraically independent?', stated with AlgebraicIndependent ℚ ![e, π].
The file formalizes the 'Open questions on irrationality of numbers' from the Wikipedia article Irrational number; this decl asks 'Is e + pi irrational?'.
Docstring asks 'Is the Euler-Mascheroni constant gamma irrational?', stated as Irrational eulerMascheroniConstant.
Docstring asks 'Is the Catalan constant G = sum (-1)^n/(2n+1)^2 irrational?', stated as Irrational catalanConstant.

Docstring states 'The **Jacobian Conjecture**: any regular function kⁿ → kᵐ whose Jacobian is a non-zero constant has an inverse given by a regular function', matching the Wikipedia reference Jacobian conjecture.

The **Juggler Conjecture** states that every juggler sequence (iterating jugglerStep from any positive integer) reaches 1; the file references the Wikipedia article Juggler sequence, whose Wikidata item is 'sequence hypothesized to always terminate at 1'.

The Kakeya set conjecture is stated as 'Kakeya sets in R^n have Hausdorff dimension n', over IsKakeya sets containing a unit segment in every direction; header references the Wikipedia article Kakeya set.
The conjecture asserts every Kakeya set 'has Hausdorff dimension n' (dimH S = n).
The solved variant is 'A finite field variant of the Kakeya problem' over subsets of F_q^n, proved by Dvir with the Bukh-Chao bound.

The file header references the Wikipedia article Kaplansky's conjectures; this decl states 'The zero-divisor conjecture: if G is torsion-free, the group algebra K[G] has no non-trivial zero divisors'.
The conjecture is about zero divisors in 'the group algebra K[G]' (MonoidAlgebra K G), i.e. the group ring of a torsion-free group.
This decl states 'The idempotent conjecture: if G is torsion-free, then K[G] has no non-trivial idempotents', one of Kaplansky's conjectures per the referenced article.
This decl states 'The **Unit Conjecture** is false', citing Gardam/Murray/Passman counterexamples to Kaplansky's unit conjecture in the group ring of the Promislow group.
Docstring states 'The **Köthe conjecture**: In any ring, the sum of two nil left ideals is nil', with the file header referencing the Wikipedia article Köthe conjecture.
An equivalent formulation labeled 'The **Köthe conjecture**: every left nil radical is contained in the Köthe radical'.
An equivalent formulation labeled 'The **Köthe conjecture**: for any nil ideal I of R, the matrix ideal M_n(I) is a nil ideal of M_n(R)'.
An equivalent formulation labeled 'The **Köthe conjecture**: for any nil ideal I of R, the matrix ideal M_2(I) is a nil ideal of M_2(R)'.
An equivalent formulation labeled 'The **Köthe conjecture**: for any positive integer n, the Köthe radical of R is the matrix ideal M_2(Nil*(R))'.
The conjecture is entirely about nil ideals: 'the sum of two nil left ideals is nil', with IsNil defined as every element nilpotent.

Header describes 'The Komlós conjecture in discrepancy theory' and references the Discrepancy theory Wikipedia article's Major open problems section; no dedicated Wikidata item for the Komlós conjecture exists.

Docstring states 'Kummer–Vandiver conjecture states that for every prime p, the class number of the maximal real subfield of Q(zeta_p) is not divisible by p', matching the referenced Wikipedia article.
The statement concerns 'the maximal real subfield of Q(zeta_p)', formalized via CyclotomicField p ℚ.
The statement is that p does not divide 'the class number' (classNumber, the order of the ideal class group) of the maximal real subfield.
